name: sema4ai-data
documentation:
# ⚡️ sema4ai-data

Python library to develop data packages for Sema4.ai.

Note: This library is currently under heavy development and APIs are expected to be broken (until a 1.0.0 release).

License: See [LICENSE](Sema4.ai End User License Agreement)

functions.name: sema4ai.data._sql_handling.build_query_from_dict_params
documentation:
Replace all the $name in the query with the actual values from params.

Args:
    query: The SQL query with placeholders.
    params: A dictionary of parameters to inject into the query.

Returns:
    str: The query with parameters injected.

functions.name: sema4ai.data._sql_handling.build_query_from_list_params
documentation:
Replace all the ? in the query with the actual values from params.

Args:
    query: The SQL query with placeholders.
    params: A list of parameters to inject into the query.

Returns:
    str: The query with parameters injected.

methods.name: sema4ai.data._result_set.ResultSet.as_dataframe
documentation: Converts the result set to a pandas DataFrame.

methods.name: sema4ai.data._result_set.ResultSet.iter_as_dicts
documentation: Iterates over the result set as a list of dictionaries.

methods.name: sema4ai.data._result_set.ResultSet.iter_as_tuples
documentation: Iterates over the result set values as a list of tuples.

methods.name: sema4ai.data._result_set.ResultSet.build_list
documentation:
Builds a list of objects of the given class from the result set.

Args:
    item_class: The class to build the list of.

Returns:
    A list of objects of the given class.

Note: The class must have a constructor that accepts each item in the
result set as keyword arguments.

methods.name: sema4ai.data._result_set.ResultSet.to_markdown
documentation: Converts the result set to a markdown table.

documentation: The name of the datasource.

methods.name: sema4ai.data._data_source.DataSource.connection
documentation: The connection to the data server.

methods.name: sema4ai.data._data_source.DataSource.model_validate
documentation:
Creates a DataSource given its name.

Return: A DataSource instance with the given value.

Note: the model_validate method is used for compatibility with
    the pydantic API.

params.name: list[str | int | float] | dict[str, str | int | float]
list[str | int | float] | dict[str, str | int | float]

documentation:
DataSource refers to a given source configured in the data server.

i.e.: In the data server, it's possible to configure a postgres connection
with a name like "my_postgres_datasource". Then, this class can be used
to refer to that datasource (without having to explicitly add the name of
the datasource in the written SQL afterwards).

classes.name: sema4ai.data._data_source._DataSourceImpl
documentation: Actual implementation of DataSource (not exposed as we can tweak as needed, only the public API should be relied upon).

functions.name: sema4ai.data.query
documentation:
Decorator for queries which can be executed by `sema4ai.actions`.

i.e.:

If a file such as actions.py has the contents below:

```python
from sema4ai.data import query

@query
def query_invoices(date: str, datasource: DataSource[Literal["invoices-datasource"]]) -> list[str]:
    ...
```

Note that a query needs sema4ai actions to be executed.
The command line to execute it is:

python -m sema4ai.actions run actions.py -a query_invoices

Args:
    func: A function which is a query to `sema4ai.data`.
    is_consequential: Whether the action is consequential or not.
        This will add `x-openai-isConsequential: true` to the action
        metadata and shown in OpenApi spec.
    display_name: A name to be displayed for this action.
        If given will be used as the openapi.json summary for this action.

functions.name: sema4ai.data.predict
documentation:
Decorator for predictions based on data models which can be executed by `sema4ai.actions`.

i.e.:

If a file such as actions.py has the contents below:

```python
from sema4ai.data import predict

@predict
def cashflow_prediction(scenario: str, datasource: DataSource[Literal["reports-datasource"]]) -> list[str]:
    ...
```

Note that a prediction needs sema4ai actions to be executed.
The command line to execute it is:

python -m sema4ai.actions run actions.py -a cashflow_prediction

Args:
    func: A function which is a prediction to `sema4ai.data`.
    is_consequential: Whether the action is consequential or not.
        This will add `x-openai-isConsequential: true` to the action
        metadata and shown in OpenApi spec.
    display_name: A name to be displayed for this action.
        If given will be used as the openapi.json summary for this action.

methods.name: sema4ai.data._data_server_connection.DataServerConnection.query

params.name: dict[str, str | int | float] | list[str | int | float]

documentation:
Simple API to query a database in MindsDB with parameters. Always loads
all the results into memory.

Args:
    database_name: The name of the database to query.
    query: The SQL query to execute.
    params: A list of parameters to inject into the query.

Returns:
    ResultSet: The query result as a ResultSet.

modules.name: sema4ai.data._connection_provider
documentation:
The code below could be better. It's a bit too over engineered for what it does
-- it's this way mostly due to historical reasons as initially it was per DataSource and
it was then extracted, so, it could be simplified in the future....
